lever: replace dense full-spectrum eigvalsh (O(9-22 n^3)) with deflated trace-of-inverse K = trace((I-H+v0 v0^T)^-1)-1 via Cholesky + batched solve (O(2/3..2 n^3)); size gate n>=256 keeps small graphs on eigvalsh (bit-exact to nx, faster there)
math: H = D^-1/2 A D^-1/2 symmetric normalized adjacency; lambda=1 simple (connected) with eigenvector v0=sqrt(deg)/||sqrt(deg)|| exact for weighted+self-loops; M=I-H+v0 v0^T is SPD with eigenvalues {1} U {1-lambda_i}; trace(M^-1)=1+K

parity.coverage: weighted, self-loops, bipartite, gate boundary (256), n up to 1000; small graphs bit-exact via eigvalsh path

headline: kemeny_constant dense eigvalsh -> Cholesky trace-of-inverse: 1.67-11.7x faster for n>=300, machine-exact (rel 2e-12), no small-graph regression. Pure Python.
note: old fnx was verbatim nx (both dense eigvalsh), so nx timing == fnx-before
